sections.paragraph: an unused root, softened from גָּרַר (like חָרַל from חָרַר to burn), having the sense of roughness, especially used of a rough, gravelly, gritty soil, on which the foot gives forth a scraping, grating sound; hence Arab. جَرِلُ a gravelly place, جَرَلُ gravel. Hence Heb. גֹּורָל pr. a little stone, hence a lot, Gr. transp. ΚΛηΡος, Lat. GLaRea, and Arab. جَرِلَ to be stony, Kamûs p. 1412, which is derived from the noun جَرَلُ stones, whence also جَرِلُ a stony place.
